items.source_story_title: Show HN: Faceoff – A terminal UI for following NHL games
items.name: Playball
items.summary: A terminal app for following MLB games.
items.why_included: A direct inspiration for another terminal sports scoreboard app.
items.evidence: The Faceoff author said the project was inspired by Playball, a similar terminal application for following MLB games.

items.source_story_title: Notion leaks email addresses of all editors of any public page
items.name: Notion
items.summary: A note workspace praised for heavy use and integrations.
items.why_included: Strong positive endorsement in a thread about a widely used product.
items.evidence: A commenter said they love Notion, use it heavily, and have built integrations for it.

items.source_story_title: Notion leaks email addresses of all editors of any public page
items.name: Outline
items.summary: A self-hosted Notion alternative for docs and wikis.
items.why_included: Recommended as a capable self-hosted replacement.
items.evidence: A commenter said Outline has everything they could ask for from a Notion alternative.

items.source_story_title: Notion leaks email addresses of all editors of any public page
items.name: Trilium
items.summary: An open-source note app with SQLite and automatic backups.
items.why_included: Recommended as a robust self-hosted option without the collaboration layer.
items.evidence: The comment explicitly recommended Trilium and called out SQLite plus daily and weekly backups.

items.source_story_title: Notion leaks email addresses of all editors of any public page
items.name: Docmost
items.summary: A self-hosted Notion and Confluence alternative.
items.why_included: Positive self-hosted alternative with clear product fit.
items.evidence: The author said Docmost is open-source, easy to self-host, and feature-packed.

items.source_story_title: College instructor turns to typewriters to curb AI-written work
items.name: writerdeckOS
items.summary: A laptop-to-typewriter OS for distraction-free writing.
items.why_included: A concrete personal endorsement from someone actually using it.
items.evidence: A commenter said they flashed an old MacBook Air with writerdeckOS and it feels like a digital typewriter.

items.source_story_title: Show HN: Shader Lab, like Photoshop but for shaders
items.name: Shader Lab
items.summary: A Photoshop-like web playground for shaders.
items.why_included: Explicitly recommended alongside another shader resource.
items.evidence: A commenter said to check out Shader Lab and shaders.com for creative shader effects.

items.source_story_title: SPEAKE(a)R: Turn Speakers to Microphones for Fun and Profit [pdf] (2017)

items.summary: An ALSA utility for retasking audio jacks.
items.why_included: Direct tool recommendation in a hardware-hack discussion.
items.evidence: A comment said to use hdajackretask from alsa-tools to retask the jacks.

items.source_story_title: Anonymous request-token comparisons from Opus 4.6 and Opus 4.7

items.summary: A minimal terminal coding harness with extensions and skills.
items.why_included: Named as a strong alternative in the model-comparison thread.

items.summary: Secure cloud sandboxes for AI agents and code execution.
items.why_included: A practical platform mentioned positively in a browser-demo thread.
items.evidence: A commenter said they used E2B to do some basic research.

items.summary: A train simulator recommended in the Japan railways thread.

items.summary: Google’s open model family for lightweight AI apps.
items.why_included: Praised directly in a comment about browser-based model demos.
items.evidence: A commenter said the Gemma models really are amazing.

items.source_story_title: Archive of BYTE magazine, starting with issue #1 in 1975
items.name: BYTE magazine archive
items.summary: A complete BYTE magazine scan archive starting in 1975.
items.why_included: The archive triggered nostalgic praise and learning memories.
items.evidence: A commenter called BYTE their favourite magazine and said they learned a tremendous deal from it.

items.source_story_title: The seven programming ur-languages (2022)
items.name: Common Lisp: A Gentle Introduction to Symbolic Computation
items.summary: A beginner-friendly Common Lisp book for symbolic computation.
items.why_included: Recommended as a starting point for Lisp learning.
items.evidence: The comment suggested starting with Common Lisp: A Gentle Introduction to Symbolic Computation.

items.source_story_title: The seven programming ur-languages (2022)
items.name: Starting Forth
items.summary: A classic book for learning Forth.
items.why_included: Explicitly recommended as a core Forth resource.
items.evidence: A commenter named Starting Forth as an ANS-version recommendation.

items.source_story_title: The seven programming ur-languages (2022)
items.name: PAIP
items.summary: Peter Norvig’s AI programming textbook and code companion.
items.why_included: Suggested as the natural next step after foundational Lisp/Forth books.
items.evidence: A commenter said they would jump into PAIP, Paradigms of AI Programming.

items.source_story_title: The seven programming ur-languages (2022)
items.name: Simply Scheme
items.summary: A beginner-friendly Scheme textbook.
items.why_included: Recommended as one of the newbie books before SICP.
items.evidence: The comment listed Simply Scheme among the starter Scheme books.

items.source_story_title: Nanopass Framework: Clean Compiler Creation Language
items.name: Nanopass Framework
items.summary: A compiler framework for clean language implementation.
items.why_included: A direct positive endorsement from someone who used it in grad school.
items.evidence: A commenter said they used the Nanopass framework and loved it.

items.source_story_title: Why Japan has such good railways

items.summary: Donald Shoup’s parking policy book and preface PDF.
items.why_included: Strongly recommended as a useful urban-policy read.
items.evidence: A commenter pointed readers to the free preface PDF for The High Price of Free Parking.

items.source_story_title: The electromechanical angle computer inside the B-52 bomber's star tracker
